description: Passing a secret through a build ARG or RUN, so it persists in an intermediate image layer even if deleted later.
body: Failure mode: a token or key is supplied via ARG or echoed in a RUN to fetch private dependencies, so it is recorded in that layer's history and remains extractable from the image even after a later RUN deletes the file. Detection signals: image history shows the secret in a build ARG or command; a layer scan finds credential strings; the secret works when pulled from the published image. Do not rely on deleting a secret in a subsequent layer. Safer fallback: use BuildKit --mount=type=secret so the secret is never written to a layer, or fetch dependencies in a discarded builder stage; rotate any secret that already shipped. Risk boundary: treat a leaked build secret as compromised and escalate rather than patching the Dockerfile alone.
